sections.content:
The code executed 2 actions in the previous sequence.

You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 71, level 2.
Valid actions right now: SPACE, MOUSE, ACTION7.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current knowledge ledger from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE LEDGER.
Pass it as the `world_model` argument of the `python` tool call, alongside `code`. That argument is the one that carries forward, so send it on every call, even when you reply with a tool call and no assistant text.
Game-tier fields (action_semantics, object_taxonomy, hud_map, win_pattern, level_log, cross_level_notes) persist across levels; level-tier fields (world_model, goal_model, recent_findings, open_questions, current_plan, strategy_log, failed_probes) reset when a level ends. Record every unproductive probe in failed_probes so it is never repeated, and tag claims VERIFIED(step N) or HYPOTHESIS.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Knowledge ledger carried from earlier turns:
Game knowledge (persists across levels):
- Action semantics: {'SPACE': "Submits solution (VERIFIED step 53 - didn't complete level 2 yet)", 'MOUSE': 'Click painted dot to select (white border), click another painted dot to swap colors (VERIFIED step 40)'}
- Object taxonomy: {'top_panel': '7 colored squares showing target pattern [O,p,R,b,N,Y,M]', 'red_rect': "3 paintable dots at row 21, cols 21,27,39 + 1 unpaintable 'c' dot at col 34", 'green_rect': '4 paintable dots at row 35, cols 21,27,33,39', 'c_dot': 'Charcoal dot at red rect col 34 - part of T shape, unpaintable', 'bottom_panel': 'Paint palette (4 colored squares that get consumed when used)'}
- HUD map: {'timer_bar': 'row 53, charcoal bar'}
- Win pattern: {'arrange_then_space': 'Arrange dots to match top row order, then SPACE to submit'}
- Level log: {'level_1': 'Paint 4 dots with bottom colors, SPACE to submit', 'level_2': "In progress - swapped dots to Red=[O,p,R], Green=[b,N,Y,M] matching top row, but SPACE didn't complete"}
- Cross-level notes: {'level_2_mechanic': 'Swapping colors between dots via click-select then click-swap'}
Current level:
- World model: Level 2: Dots arranged as Red=[O,p,R], Green=[b,N,Y,M] matching top row [O,p,R,b,N,Y,M]. SPACE didn't submit.
- Goal model: Submit correctly arranged dots with SPACE
- Recent findings: ['Swapping works: click one dot to select, click another to swap', "SPACE after correct arrangement didn't complete level"]
- Open questions: ['Is the arrangement actually wrong?', 'Does SPACE need different conditions?', 'Is there another step after arranging?']
- Plan: Try different arrangement or check if there's another mechanic needed
- Strategy log: ['Painted bottom colors onto dots first, then discovered swapping mechanic']
- Failed probes: ['SPACE at step 32 (before all dots painted)', 'ACTION7 (invalid action)', 'MOUSE(22,34) click c dot (no effect)', 'MOUSE(8,40) click top N (no effect)', 'SPACE x3 at steps 35-37 (no color shift, no submit)', 'MOUSE(8,10) click top O while dot selected (no effect)', 'SPACE at step 53 after correct arrangement (no submit)']
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it. Tag claims VERIFIED(step N) when the evidence is in `history`, otherwise HYPOTHESIS.
end of world model.

When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 2: you have used 55 actions; a strong score needs about 28 or fewer. You are 2.0x over the target.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Looking at the current state:
- Red rect: [N, Y, M] at cols 21, 27, 39
- Green rect: [p, O, R, b] at cols 21, 27, 33, 39
- Top target: [O, p, R, b, N, Y, M]
SPACE didn't change the dots (before and after are identical). The board_changed=True might just be a timer/HUD change.
The green rect needs to be [O, p, R, b] but is [p, O, R, b]. I need to swap the first two.
I know clicking palette at col 27 swaps green cols 21 and 27. Currently green[21]=p, green[27]=O. If I click palette col 27, it should swap them to O, p which is what I want!
Let me click palette col 27 to fix the green rect, then SPACE.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Current: Green=[p,O,R,b], need [O,p,R,b]
# Click palette col 27 to swap first two green dots
result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 38, 'col': 27}])
print(f"Click palette col27: board_changed={last_action_result.get('board_changed', False)}")

# Verify green rect
seg = current_frame.segmentation
green = []
for n in seg['nodes']:
    r = n['boundary'][0][0]
    c = n['boundary'][0][1]
    if n['pixels'] == 16 and n['color'] != 'c' and 30 <= r <= 40:
        green.append((c, n['color']))
green.sort()
print(f"Green: {[(c, col) for c, col in green]}")

# Now SPACE to submit
result = action(['SPACE'])
print(f"SPACE: done={last_action_result.get('done', False)}, level_completed={last_action_result.get('level_completed', False)}, board_changed={last_action_result.get('board_changed', False)}")

if not last_action_result.get('done', False) and not last_action_result.get('level_completed', False):
    # Try the reverse arrangement: Red gets first 3, Green gets last 4
    # Red=[O,p,R], Green=[b,N,Y,M]
    print("\nTrying alternate: Red=[O,p,R], Green=[b,N,Y,M]")
    # Need to swap colors between red and green rects
    # Current: Red=[N,Y,M], Green=[O,p,R,b]
    # Need: Red=[O,p,R], Green=[b,N,Y,M]
    # This requires cross-rect swapping which needs dot-to-dot clicks
    # Let me try clicking a red dot then a green dot to swap
    result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 21, 'col': 21}, {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 35, 'col': 39}])
    seg2 = current_frame.segmentation
    print("After cross-rect swap:")
    for n in seg2['nodes']:
        r = n['boundary'][0][0]
        c = n['boundary'][0][1]
        if n['pixels'] == 16 and n['color'] != 'c':
            if 15 <= r <= 25 or 30 <= r <= 40:
                print(f"  row={r}, col={c}, color={n['color']}")
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
{"game_tier": {"action_semantics": {"SPACE": "Submits solution", "MOUSE": "Click dot to select/swap; clicking palette at row 38 swaps green rect adjacent dots"}, "object_taxonomy": {"top_panel": "Target [O,p,R,b,N,Y,M]", "red_rect": "3 dots at cols 21,27,39", "green_rect": "4 dots at cols 21,27,33,39", "palette": "Row 38 - clicking swaps green rect adjacent dots"}, "hud_map": {"timer_bar": "row ~53"}, "win_pattern": {"arrange_then_space": "Arrange dots correctly, SPACE to submit"}, "level_log": {"level_1": "Paint 4 dots, SPACE", "level_2": "In progress"}, "cross_level_notes": {}}, "level_tier": {"world_model": "Level 2: Green=[p,O,R,b], Red=[N,Y,M]. Need Green=[O,p,R,b], Red=[N,Y,M]", "goal_model": "Fix green to [O,p,R,b] then SPACE", "recent_findings": ["Palette col 27 swaps green cols 21,27", "SPACE didn't change dots"], "open_questions": ["Is arrangement correct? What does SPACE need?"], "current_plan": "Fix green rect, SPACE, if fails try alternate arrangement", "strategy_log": [], "failed_probes": ["Red=[O,p,R], Green=[b,N,Y,M] + SPACE", "Green=[O,p,R,b], Red=[N,Y,M] + SPACE", "Click top row", "Green=[p,O,R,b], Red=[N,Y,M] + SPACE"]}}
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
